message.content:
Find all files related to "womens-opt" or "hormone-imbalance" in this WordPress theme repo. Look for:
1. PHP page templates (page-*.php files)
2. SCSS files in src/static/scss/pages/
3. JS bundle files in src/static/js/pages/
4. Any rewrite rules or URL routing in functions.php related to womens-opt
5. The HONE_PAGE_TEMPLATES array in functions.php

Also look for any existing "enhanced PDP" hero patterns - search for "enhanced-pdp", "enhanced_pdp", "gif hero", "hero-gif" in PHP and SCSS files. Check the .local/figma-to-code/ directory for ENHANCED_PDP_NAMING.md.

Be thorough - check multiple naming conventions.

content.text: I'll help you find all files related to "womens-opt", "hormone-imbalance", and "enhanced-pdp" patterns. Let me search systematically across the codebase.

1→# Enhanced PDP Naming Conventions 2→ 3→Observed patterns from `page-mens-hrt-enhanced-pdp-1.php`. Use this as the authoritative reference for class naming — it reflects what's actually in production templates. 4→ 5→> **Note:** `SECTION_LIBRARY.md` uses BEM-style naming (`hero__title`, `hero__content`) as an abstraction layer. Actual templates use **flat compound naming** instead. Follow the flat style documented here. 6→ 7→--- 8→ 9→## Section Class Naming 10→ 11→Sections use a **primary descriptive name** plus optional **modifier utility classes**: 12→ 13→```php 14→
15→``` 16→ 17→### Examples from this template 18→ 19→| Primary Name | Modifier Classes | Notes | 20→|---|---|---| 21→| `boost-your-testosterone hero` | `border-bottom` | Two semantic names on one section | 22→| `marquee trustpilot-ratings` | `border-bottom extended-center-border-below` | | 23→| `how-it-works social-videos` | `extended-center-border-below` | | 24→| `benefits-timeline` | `border-bottom extended-center-border-above` | | 25→| `proven-benefits` | `border-bottom extended-center-border-above` | | 26→| `injections-section` | `border-bottom extended-center-border-below` | Has `id="injections-section"` for anchor | 27→| `testimonials` | `extended-center-border-below` | | 28→| `alternatives` | `extended-center-border-above` | | 29→ 30→### Section Border Modifier Classes 31→ 32→These control the decorative center-line border between sections: 33→ 34→| Class | Effect | 35→|---|---| 36→| `border-bottom` | Adds a bottom border to the section | 37→| `extended-center-border-below` | Extends a vertical center border downward into the next section | 38→| `extended-center-border-above` | Extends a vertical center border upward into the previous section | 39→ 40→These can be combined. Typical pairings: 41→- `border-bottom extended-center-border-below` — section ends with border that continues down 42→- `border-bottom extended-center-border-above` — section receives border from above 43→ 44→--- 45→ 46→## Container/Wrapper/Block Vocabulary 47→ 48→Consistent suffix vocabulary used throughout all templates: 49→ 50→| Suffix | Role | Example | 51→|---|---|---| 52→| `.container` | Outermost layout container inside `
` | `
` | 53→| `.container.grid` | Grid layout variant | `
` | 54→| `.container.grid.grid-2-col` | 2-column grid | `
` | 55→| `*-wrapper` | Grouping container for a region | `.media-wrapper`, `.content-wrapper`, `.title-wrapper`, `.carousel-wrapper`, `.badges-wrapper` | 56→| `*-block` | A self-contained content unit | `.title-block`, `.content-block`, `.media-block`, `.list-block`, `.cta-block`, `.grid-block`, `.faq-block` | 57→| `*-item` or `*-card` | Repeating unit within a loop | `.review-item`, `.testimonial-item`, `.faq-item`, `.timeline-card`, `.benefits-item`, `.grid-item` | 58→ 59→### Typical Section Structure 60→ 61→```php 62→

76→``` 77→ 78→--- 79→ 80→## Responsive Visibility Classes 81→ 82→```php 83→class="desktop-only" // Hidden on tablet and smaller 84→class="tablet-and-smaller-only" // Hidden on desktop 85→``` 86→ 87→Used in `injections-section` to swap between a static desktop grid and a mobile carousel for the same image set. 88→ 89→--- 90→ 91→## Grid Classes on `.container` 92→ 93→```php 94→
// Basic grid 95→
// Two-column grid 96→``` 97→ 98→Column children can have `.border-right` to add a divider: 99→```php 100→
101→``` 102→ 103→--- 104→ 105→## Carousel Pattern (Actual) 106→ 107→The `.container` child gets both a generic `.carousel` class and a **prefix-specific** class. The JS utility picks it up by prefix. 108→ 109→```php 110→
111→

133→``` 134→ 135→```javascript 136→initCarousel({}, '.[prefix]'); 137→``` 138→ 139→--- 140→ 141→## FAQ Icon Pattern (Actual) 142→ 143→The actual template uses **two `` elements** for the +/× animation, NOT a plain `+` text character: 144→ 145→```php 146→ 147→ 148→ 149→ 150→``` 151→ 152→The `.line-vertical` is rotated/hidden via CSS on expand. 153→ 154→--- 155→ 156→## `render_cta_block()` Signature 157→ 158→```php 159→render_cta_block($url, $label, $secondary = false, $extra_class = ''); 160→ 161→// Scroll to anchor on same page: 162→render_cta_block('#injections-section', 'Test my T-Levels'); 163→ 164→// Checkout URL, default primary style: 165→render_cta_block($checkout_url, 'Test My T-Levels'); 166→ 167→// With custom class on the wrapper: 168→render_cta_block($checkout_url, 'Test My T-Levels', false, 'proven-benefits-cta-block'); 169→``` 170→ 171→--- 172→ 173→## Script Enqueuing with JS Data 174→ 175→When a template needs to pass PHP data to JavaScript (e.g., `themeFolder`), use `add_action` + `wp_localize_script` instead of a direct enqueue call: 176→ 177→```php 178→add_action('wp_enqueue_scripts', function() use ($template_name, $theme_folder) { 179→ wp_enqueue_script('hone-page-' . $template_name); 180→ wp_localize_script('hone-page-' . $template_name, 'honePageData', array( 181→ 'themeFolder' => $theme_folder 182→ )); 183→}); 184→``` 185→ 186→In JS, access via `window.honePageData.themeFolder`. 187→ 188→--- 189→ 190→## `$additional_disclaimers` Global 191→ 192→For page-specific legal disclaimers appended in the footer: 193→ 194→```php 195→global $class_names, $additional_disclaimers; 196→ 197→$additional_disclaimers = [ 198→ 'Results may vary. Medications are prescribed if deemed to be clinically necessary...' 199→]; 200→``` 201→ 202→--- 203→ 204→## Sections in This Template (Reference) 205→ 206→Ordered as they appear on the page: 207→ 208→1. **`boost-your-testosterone hero`** — Video autoplay + content split with bullet list, badges 209→2. **`marquee trustpilot-ratings`** — Trustpilot static badge + CSS marquee of short review quotes 210→3. **`how-it-works social-videos`** — Carousel of short-form review videos with play/mute controls 211→4. **`benefits-timeline`** — Horizontal scroll carousel of day-milestone cards (Day 7/30/60/90) 212→5. **`proven-benefits`** — 2-col grid: image left, 3×2 icon benefits grid right + CTA 213→6. **`injections-section`** — Product gallery (desktop grid / mobile thumbs carousel) + content + inline FAQ 214→7. **`testimonials`** — Testimonial carousel with star ratings, nav arrows + dots 215→8. **`alternatives`** — Product alternatives grid (oral troches, cream) + CTA 216→

### **HONE_PAGE_TEMPLATES Array in functions.php**

The following templates are registered in `/Users/jbiggs/code/buy-hh/buy-honehealth-com/themes/HoneLandingPages/src/functions.php` (lines 23-68):

**Women's Templates:**
- `'womens-hormone-imbalance'` (line 61)

**Enhanced PDP Templates:**

- `'mens-opt-enhanced-pdp'` (line 42)

### **Enhanced PDP Reference Documentation**

**Main Resource:**
- `/Users/jbiggs/code/buy-hh/.local/figma-to-code/ENHANCED_PDP_NAMING.md` — Comprehensive naming conventions and patterns reference

**Related Documentation in .local/figma-to-code/:**
- `DESIGN_SYSTEM.md` — Design system standards
- `NEW_TEMPLATE_PATTERN.md` — Template creation patterns
- `QUICK_REFERENCE.md` — Quick copy-paste code templates
- `SECTION_LIBRARY.md` — Section component patterns
- `RESPONSIVE_PATTERNS.md` — Responsive design patterns
- `FIGMA_WORKFLOW.md` — Figma to code workflow
- `README.md` — Directory overview

### **Build Output (Compiled Files)**

All templates have corresponding compiled CSS and minified JS in the `/build/` directory:
- `/Users/jbiggs/code/buy-hh/buy-honehealth-com/themes/HoneLandingPages/build/static/css/pages/` — Contains `.css` and `.css.map` files
- `/Users/jbiggs/code/buy-hh/buy-honehealth-com/themes/HoneLandingPages/build/static/js/dist/pages/` — Contains `.bundle.min.js` files

### **Key Findings**

1. **No "gif-hero" or "hero-gif" patterns found** — The enhanced-pdp templates use `<video autoplay muted loop>` elements instead of GIF files for hero sections
2. **All template naming follows consistent conventions** — PHP slug matches SCSS filename, JS filename, and HONE_PAGE_TEMPLATES entry
3. **Two enhanced-pdp patterns exist** — One for mens-opt and one for mens-hrt, using identical architectural approaches
4. **Women's hormone templates use quiz-based UX** — Both `womens-opt-light-touch` and `womens-hormone-imbalance*` variants include interactive quiz sections
5. **Additional disclaimers pattern** — Enhanced-pdp templates use `$additional_disclaimers` global for medical/legal notices
6. **Video autoplay pattern** — Enhanced-pdp templates implement autoplay with `wp_localize_script` to pass theme folder paths to JS
